---
title: "Tier 1 — Foundations"
subtitle: "Grade 5–7 · Number sense, the four operations re-grounded, fractions, ratio, geometry, and the seed of every later idea"
---

## What this tier is for

Tier 1 is the bedrock. Whether you arrived here as an eleven-year-old, an
adult who never quite trusted their grasp of fractions, or a software engineer
who wants the encryption door to open later — this tier gives you the
*understanding* of why every operation works, not just how to push the symbols.

By the end of Tier 1 you can:

- Place any number — whole, fraction, or decimal — on a number line and say
  what it means.
- Reason about ratios and proportions in real situations.
- Compute area, perimeter, and volume of basic shapes from first principles.
- Read and write simple algebraic expressions.
- Tell whether a number is prime, and explain why that question matters
  (it is the door to modern encryption).

## The lessons

The arrows show prerequisites — if a lesson points to another, the second
needs the first. You can take any path through the graph; the order below is
the recommended linear walk.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A[1. What is a Number?] --> B[2. The Number Line]
    A --> C[3. Place Value]
    B --> D[4. Add & Subtract, Re-grounded]
    C --> D
    D --> E[5. Multiplication as Area]
    E --> F[6. Division as Fair Share]
    F --> G[7. Fractions as Parts]
    G --> H[8. Decimals]
    C --> H
    G --> I[9. Ratio & Proportion]
    B --> J[10. Negative Numbers]
    D --> J
    D --> K[11. Variables]
    E --> K
    L[12. Shapes & Angles] --> M[13. Area, Perimeter, Volume]
    E --> M
    F --> N[14. Data & Average]
    F --> O[15. Divisibility & Primes]

    classDef done fill:#c8e6c9,stroke:#2e7d32
    class A,B,C done
```

## Lesson list

1. [**What is a Number?**](01-what-is-a-number/index.qmd) — *the exemplar lesson.*
2. [**The Number Line**](02-number-line/index.qmd) — *built.*
3. [**Place Value — Why Position Matters**](03-place-value/index.qmd) — *built.*
4. Addition and Subtraction, Re-grounded — *planned*
5. Multiplication as Area — *planned*
6. Division as Fair Share — *planned*
7. Fractions as Parts of a Whole — *planned*
8. Decimals — Fractions in a New Costume — *planned*
9. Ratio and Proportion — *planned*
10. Negative Numbers — *planned*
11. Variables — Names for Unknown Numbers — *planned*
12. Shapes and Angles — *planned*
13. Area, Perimeter, Volume — *planned*
14. Data and the Average — *planned*
15. Divisibility and Primes (the Encryption Seed) — *planned*

::: {.callout-note}
This page reflects the snapshot of `_curriculum.yml` at the time of build.
Lesson statuses there are the source of truth.
:::
